summary: metrics-pipeline-d8k2 ships postinstall beacon that exfiltrates host info over HTTP
details: The package's package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node run.js") that triggers automatically on npm install. The tarball ships beacon scripts (beacon34.js and beacon_linux.js) that combine child_process, http/https, os.hostname()/os.platform(), and process.env reads with outbound HTTP GET/POST requests — the canonical install-time host-reconnaissance + exfiltration shape. beacon_linux.js issues http.get/http.request calls (lines 94, 144, 282–283) against host data gathered from os.hostname() and os.platform() (lines 55, 58, 73). beacon34.js similarly pulls in child_process and https, reads process.env (lines 58, 69) and process.platform (line 56), and issues outbound GETs (lines 37, 99, 110, 112). The package name itself is a generic-sounding lure with a random suffix (-d8k2), with no legitimate library content commensurate with the shipped beacons. Installing this package causes the installer's machine to send environment variables and host fingerprint data to an attacker-controlled destination at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
